subject: Geology
title: Can We Predict Earthquakes?
description: One-minute skill: Can We Predict Earthquakes?.

hook.text: Despite decades of research, no one can predict the exact time, place, and size of an earthquake. Not yet.

buildup.text: Prediction requires specifying when, where, and how big a quake will be, with enough lead time to act on it.

discovery.text: Attempts using animal behavior, radon gas, and electromagnetic signals have all failed to produce reliable forecasts.

twist.text: Probabilistic forecasting is possible. We can say a region has a 60% chance of a magnitude 7+ quake in 30 years.

climax.text: Prediction remains the holy grail of seismology. Until we achieve it, preparation is our strongest tool.

punchline.text: We can't predict, but we can prepare.

quiz.question: Can scientists currently predict the exact time of an earthquake?
options: No, but probabilistic forecasting is possible
